icp_name: Dual ICP — Executive Health Advisory (Aligness) + Dental Professional Training (Glossodontics)
profile_purpose: Inform content creation, lead magnet design, and advisory/training positioning across both business units

primary_icp_aligness.segment_name: The Executive with the Oral-Systemic Blind Spot
primary_icp_aligness.description: High-performing executive, founder, or PE partner who has invested significantly in health optimization — functional medicine, executive physicals, biohacking — but whose health team has never included someone who understands the mouth-body connection. Their brain fog, disrupted sleep, and chronic fatigue have a systemic oral driver that no one has identified because no one thought to look.
primary_icp_aligness.primary_pain: A health team of excellent siloed specialists that has never evaluated their airway, tongue function, or oral-systemic inflammation as contributors to performance decline
primary_icp_aligness.vladimir_specific_entry_point: Brain fog or sleep disruption that cannot be explained by existing diagnoses — and that no one has connected to structural oral drivers. The CPAP that doesn't work. The fatigue that persists despite perfect sleep hygiene. The inflammation that no supplement stack resolves.

secondary_icp_glossodontics.segment_name: The Dentist Who Sees the Pattern But Lacks the Framework
secondary_icp_glossodontics.description: General or biological dentist who has been observing clinical patterns their training never explained — patients with persistent symptoms, tongue-tie presentations in adults, airway drivers behind orthodontic relapse — and who is actively seeking a systematic framework to expand their scope of care into dental-airway functional treatment.
secondary_icp_glossodontics.primary_pain: Seeing the clinical problem clearly but lacking the surgical training, protocol framework, or referral network to address it systematically
secondary_icp_glossodontics.buying_trigger: A patient case where the outcome pointed unmistakably toward tongue/airway as the driver, and the dentist realized they had no pathway to address it

executive_audience.location: US major metros + high-net-worth suburban markets

dental_professional_audience.location: US + international (Vladimir has existing international CE audience)

identity_roles: Performance optimizer who has tried everything and still has unexplained symptoms
Systems thinker who applies strategic frameworks to every domain — and is frustrated that health doesn't respond to the same logic
The leader who manages five specialists and is the only person in the room with the full picture
aspirations: Resolve the performance floor they cannot break through despite doing everything right
Have a health team that communicates and coordinates the way their business teams do
Understand the oral-systemic connection before it becomes a larger health crisis
Find the one thing everyone else missed
pain_points: Brain fog or cognitive slowdown with no diagnosable cause
Sleep apnea or chronic fatigue that CPAP has not resolved
Systemic inflammation markers that functional medicine has not been able to reduce to baseline
Dental and medical care operating in completely separate silos
Never having been told that their oral health could be driving their systemic symptoms
fears: That the explanation for their performance decline has been sitting in plain sight and nobody thought to look
That their brain fog will continue to degrade decision-making quality at the worst possible time
That a coordination failure between their specialists will eventually produce a serious outcome
belief_obstacles: Dentistry is cosmetic and structural — it does not affect energy, cognition, or systemic health
If something serious were happening orally, one of their existing doctors would have caught it
Functional medicine already addresses root causes — they do not need another practitioner type
trust_triggers: Clinical specificity — explaining the mechanism (airway → oxygen delivery → sleep architecture → brain fog) in enough detail that it cannot be dismissed as wellness marketing
International credentials — Silver Medal from European Congress of Implantologists, Diplomate ICOI, international lecturing
Patented technique — signals genuine innovation, not repackaged conventional care
Aligned co-founder — the fact that an ophthalmologist and surgeon chose to co-found with a dentist is itself a credibility signal
Glossodontics as category creation — building a training system for other dentists signals a level of expertise that goes beyond clinical practice

pain_points: Dental school never taught airway assessment, tongue-tie identification, or functional orthodontics
Patients with persistent symptoms that conventional dentistry cannot explain
No systematic referral network with ENT, sleep medicine, and myofunctional therapists
No training pathway that integrates all the relevant modalities into one coherent clinical model
aspirations: Expand scope of practice into high-demand, differentiated services
Become the dental provider in their market known for treating the whole patient
Access a systematic training framework with ongoing case support
Build referral relationships with ENT, sleep specialists, and functional medicine practitioners
buying_triggers: Attending a lecture or conference where the airway-systemic connection is demonstrated clinically
A patient case that pointed unmistakably toward tongue/airway as the driver
Seeing a peer in their market gain significant differentiation through airway-focused practice

executive_voice: My CPAP isn't working. I'm still exhausted.
I've been to every specialist. Nobody has connected the dots.
I eat clean, exercise 5 days a week, and I'm still foggy by 2pm.
Nobody told me my tongue could affect my breathing.
My endocrinologist checked my thyroid. My cardiologist checked my heart. Nobody checked my mouth.
I don't have time to be my own chief medical officer.
data_points: People with gum disease have 2-3x the risk of heart attack and stroke (American Heart Association)
Porphyromonas gingivalis — primary gum disease bacterium — has been found in brain tissue of Alzheimer's patients
Pro-inflammatory cytokines from the mouth travel through the bloodstream (Nature — Scientific Reports)
Moderate sleep deprivation impairs cognitive performance as much as being legally intoxicated
Half of all CEOs get less than six hours of sleep per night (Entrepreneur.com)
The amygdala becomes 60% more reactive when sleep-deprived
Only 49% of US primary care doctors communicate with the specialists their patients see

customer_journey.executive_awareness: Encounters Vladimir's LinkedIn content that names an unexplained symptom (brain fog, sleep disruption, fatigue) and attributes it to an oral driver they have never considered
customer_journey.executive_consideration: Downloads lead magnet (Mouth-Body Connection guide, Airway Assessment checklist), self-identifies with 3+ items
customer_journey.executive_decision: Books strategy call after recognizing that their health team has this specific blind spot and that Aligness is the only model addressing it
customer_journey.dental_awareness: Attends a Glossodontics lecture or sees Vladimir's LinkedIn content explaining the clinical pattern they have already been observing in practice
customer_journey.dental_decision: Joins Glossodontics for the systematic training framework and ongoing case support
